description: Three-section analog summing and distribution utility. Each section sums three DC-coupled inputs into three buffered outputs, with optional -6 dB attenuation. Semi-normalled chain lets it act as 1-to-9 mult or 7-to-1 mixer.

controls.name: -6 dB Switch
controls.description: Halves the post-sum signal for hot inputs.
controls.detail: up: unity · down: -6 dB · one per section

inputs.name: Section Inputs (3 per section × 3)
inputs.description: DC-coupled summing inputs. Accepts audio, CV, clock.
inputs.normalizedTo: Yellow IN 1 ← Red post-switch sum; Green IN 1 ← Yellow post-switch sum (semi-normalled chain)

outputs.name: Section Outputs (3 per section × 3)
outputs.description: Three independently buffered copies of the section sum.

behaviors.name: Three isolated summing amps
behaviors.description: Each of the red, yellow, green sections is a 3-in 3-out buffered summing amp. No cross-talk between sections unless the semi-normal is exploited — sections operate independently otherwise.

behaviors.name: Cascading semi-normal
behaviors.trigger: leave Yellow IN 1 / Green IN 1 unpatched
behaviors.description: Red's post-switch sum feeds Yellow's first input; Yellow's post-switch sum feeds Green's first input. Stacking this way turns 333 into a 1-to-9 mult (patch into Red IN 1, take 9 outs) or a 7-to-1 summing mixer (fill Red IN 1-3, Yellow IN 2-3, Green IN 2-3, take Green OUT).

behaviors.name: DC-coupled throughout
behaviors.description: Entire signal path is DC-coupled. Safe for pitch CV, envelopes, triggers, and gates — no droop, no coupling capacitor. Buffered outputs preserve 1 V/oct tracking when multing pitch.

behaviors.name: -6 dB pad placement
behaviors.description: Attenuation occurs after the summing amplifier, before the output buffers. Scales the whole section sum by 0.5, useful when summing three ±5 V modular-level signals that would otherwise clip downstream.

patchIdeas.name: 9-way pitch mult
patchIdeas.patch: Patch sequencer V/oct into Red IN 1 · leave Yellow IN 1 and Green IN 1 unpatched so the cascade propagates · all nine outputs carry the same pitch CV, each buffered independently · distribute to nine oscillators without degrading tracking.
patchIdeas.diagram:
333:
* All -6 dB: off
* Yellow IN 1, Green IN 1: unpatched (cascade active)

- Sequencer (V/oct) p> 333.Red (IN 1)
- 333.Red (OUT 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- 333.Red (OUT 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- 333.Red (OUT 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 1) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 2) p> VCO 5 (V/Oct)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 3) p> VCO 6 (V/Oct)
- 333.Green (OUT 1) p> VCO 7 (V/Oct)
- 333.Green (OUT 2) p> VCO 8 (V/Oct)
- 333.Green (OUT 3) p> VCO 9 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: 7-to-1 sum
patchIdeas.patch: Seven signals into Red IN 1-3, Yellow IN 2-3, Green IN 2-3 · cascade carries Red's sum into Yellow IN 1 and Yellow's sum into Green IN 1 · Green OUT is the running total · engage -6 dB per section as needed to keep the sum inside ±10 V.
patchIdeas.diagram:
333:
* Yellow IN 1, Green IN 1: unpatched
* -6 dB: per section as needed

- Source 1 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 1)
- Source 2 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 2)
- Source 3 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 3)
- Source 4 (Out) -> 333.Yellow (IN 2)
- Source 5 (Out) -> 333.Yellow (IN 3)
- Source 6 (Out) -> 333.Green (IN 2)
- Source 7 (Out) -> 333.Green (IN 3)
- 333.Green (OUT 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Clock merger
patchIdeas.patch: Three clocks from different dividers into Red IN 1-3 · Red OUT 1/2/3 deliver the merged pulse stream (OR-like via summing) to three destinations · DC coupling passes every rising edge cleanly.
patchIdeas.diagram:
333:

- Clock A (Out) c> 333.Red (IN 1)
- Clock B (Out) c> 333.Red (IN 2)
- Clock C (Out) c> 333.Red (IN 3)
- 333.Red (OUT 1) c> Drum 1 (Trig)
- 333.Red (OUT 2) c> Drum 2 (Trig)
- 333.Red (OUT 3) c> Drum 3 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: CV sum with headroom
patchIdeas.patch: Three LFOs summed into one composite modulation · all three into one section, -6 dB engaged so the triple-summed signal sits inside ±5 V · three buffered copies delivered to three targets at the same amplitude.
patchIdeas.diagram:
333:
* Yellow -6 dB: ON

- LFO 1 (Out) >> 333.Yellow (IN 1)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> 333.Yellow (IN 2)
- LFO 3 (Out) >> 333.Yellow (IN 3)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 2) >> VCA (CV)
- 333.Yellow (OUT 3) >> FX (Time CV)

patchIdeas.name: Three separate 3→3 groups
patchIdeas.patch: Break the semi-normal by patching Yellow IN 1 and Green IN 1 with real signals · 333 now behaves as three independent 3-in 3-out mixer-mults · red handles one voice, yellow another, green a third — one module, three utility channels.
patchIdeas.diagram:
333:
* Semi-normal broken: Yellow IN 1 and Green IN 1 patched

- Red Src 1 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 1)
- Red Src 2 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 2)
- Red Src 3 (Out) -> 333.Red (IN 3)
- Yellow Src 1 (Out) -> 333.Yellow (IN 1)
- Yellow Src 2 (Out) -> 333.Yellow (IN 2)
- Yellow Src 3 (Out) -> 333.Yellow (IN 3)
- Green Src 1 (Out) -> 333.Green (IN 1)
- Green Src 2 (Out) -> 333.Green (IN 2)
- Green Src 3 (Out) -> 333.Green (IN 3)
